Dataset name: Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children
Access type: Public metadata; study-managed raw data access
Gambling measure summary: Uploaded ALSPAC variable-name workbooks identify youth/young-adult gambling participation, product/frequency items, problem-gambling/harm items, gambling cognitions/motives, PGSI/CPGI derived variables, COVID gambling items, and G0 parent/partner gambling.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Candidate risk/protective metadata present across uploaded variable-name workbooks: mental health/wellbeing, substance use, socioeconomic position, inequalities, family, peers, school, digital environment, adversity and protective assets. Detailed variable-level risk extraction still needed.
Seed/source status: Pre-identified in evidence review; URL/variable extraction added
Notes / uncertainties: Updated from uploaded ALSPAC variable-name workbooks on 2026-04-29. Variable labels are available, but exact questionnaire wording, response coding and public variable-level URLs still need verification for many items.

Dataset name: Born in Bradford - Age of Wonder
Access type: Study-managed application; public questionnaires
Gambling measure summary: Gambling section in Age of Wonder questionnaires; wording available in questionnaires
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Mental health, wellbeing, school, family, peers, socioeconomic, ethnicity, deprivation, inequalities, digital environment
Seed/source status: Pre-identified in evidence review; URL extraction added
Notes / uncertainties: Public questionnaire links and public BiB GitHub data dictionary available; raw data access remains study-managed.

Dataset name: Millennium Cohort Study - Age 14 sweep
Access type: Public questionnaires/metadata; UKDS registered/safeguarded data access
Gambling measure summary: Longitudinal dictionary parsed for gambling-related variables, including adolescent gambling items where present.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Mental health/wellbeing, substance use, socioeconomic position/inequalities, family, peers, school, digital environment, adversity/protective assets.
Seed/source status: Pre-identified in evidence review; variable rows added
Notes / uncertainties: Uploaded longitudinal data dictionary provides variable-level metadata; questionnaire PDFs/CLOSER variable pages still needed for exact question wording and response coding.

Dataset name: Understanding Society - Wave 13 gambling module
Owner / data controller: Institute for Social and Economic Research / UK Data Service
Age focus / population: Adults and young adults within household panel; Wave 13 self-completion gambling module
Access type: Public variable search/metadata; UKDS data access
Seed/source status: Pre-identified in evidence review; variable URLs added
Notes / uncertainties: CLOSER exposes a Wave 8 gambling/betting activities code-set/category page; full variable-level extraction should use the public Understanding Society variable search before any login route.

Dataset name: School Health Research Network Student Health and Wellbeing Survey
Access type: Study-managed request / collaboration
Gambling measure summary: Past 7-day own-money gambling item; some harm/feeling-bad items in publications
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Mental health/wellbeing (WEMWBS), substance use, school, family, peers, physical activity/diet, inequalities, deprivation
Seed/source status: Pre-identified in evidence review; questionnaire URL added
Notes / uncertainties: Public questionnaire gives item number/question wording; dataset variable names likely require SHRN data request.

Dataset name: Emerging Adults Gambling Survey
Owner / data controller: Academic research team / eventual UKDS deposit noted in publications
Access type: OSF/public supplements; UKDS/data-owner route for data
Gambling measure summary: PGSI at waves; gambling, gaming, social media and health behaviours
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Mental health / suicidality, substance use, gaming, social media, socioeconomic factors (from publications/protocol)
Seed/source status: Pre-identified in evidence review; URL extraction added
Screening status: Include - seeded and confirmed by literature
Notes / uncertainties: Questionnaires/supplementary files identified through OSF and publications; full variable names not yet fully extracted from files.

Dataset name: Young People and Gambling Survey
Access type: UKDS registered access; public questionnaires/reports
Gambling measure summary: Own-money gambling participation; DSM-IV-MR-J screen in documented years; exposure/attitudes items
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Demographics, family/peer exposure, advertising/exposure, gaming/online behaviours; school/pupil characteristics likely limited
Notes / uncertainties: Strong public questionnaire/report documentation and UKDS catalogue links; historic datasets 2011-2019 and 2022-2025 are indexed.

Dataset name: Gambling Survey for Great Britain
Access type: UKDS registered access; public documentation
Gambling measure summary: Gambling participation and PGSI; product-level gambling
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Demographics, health/wellbeing, risk factors depending questionnaire; variable-level extraction needed
Notes / uncertainties: Useful for 18-24 and PGSI-related harmonisation; public questionnaire and derived-variable documentation available.

Dataset name: Health Survey for England 2021
Access type: UKDS registered access
Access difficulty: Metadata available; raw data requires UK Data Service access
Gambling measure summary: Gambling variables extracted where present from uploaded data dictionary. If none detected, dataset retained for risk/protective covariates and access metadata.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Mental health/wellbeing, substance use, socioeconomic position/inequalities, family/household, digital environment, diet/physical health variables where present.
Notes / uncertainties: Uploaded UK Data Archive data dictionary: HSE 2021 data dictionary.rtf; file hse_2021_eul_v1; 2018 variables; 7120 cases.
Source URLs: Uploaded file in current ChatGPT workspace

Dataset name: Adult Psychiatric Morbidity Survey
Access type: UKDS/NHS Digital access route to be confirmed
Gambling measure summary: PGSI/problem gambling measures in 2023/24; 2007 problem-gambling screens noted by ScotPHO; 2014 data access confirmed
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Rich psychiatric morbidity measures (CIS-R, AUDIT, ASRS, AQ, MDQ etc.), suicidality, substance use, demographics
Notes / uncertainties: High value for gambling and mental health; variable-level source requires further access/document checking.

Dataset name: National Longitudinal Study of Adolescent to Adult Health
Access type: Public-use and restricted-use data
Access difficulty: Open/registration for public; restricted for full sample
Gambling measure summary: GREO and ICPSR variable search confirm gambling questions/variables; exact wave/wording to extract
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Mental health, substance use, family, peers, school, socioeconomic, neighbourhood, biomarkers/linked contextual data
Notes / uncertainties: Navigator/codebooks support variable-level searching; restricted variables require data agreement.

Dataset name: European School Survey Project on Alcohol and Other Drugs
Access type: Data portal / application
Gambling measure summary: Gambling for money, online gambling and problematic gambling indicators in recent reports/data explorer
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Substance use, gaming/social media, mental wellbeing/prevention in 2024, socioeconomic/family variables
Screening status: Include - international high value, access unclear
Notes / uncertainties: Primarily substance-use survey; gambling items require wave/country/module confirmation.

Dataset name: Health Behaviour in School-aged Children
Access type: National team / study-specific access
Gambling measure summary: Aresi et al. paper confirms HBSC Lombardy 2021/22 gambling indicator for betting/gambling money in last 12 months and SOGS-RA adolescent gambling severity among ages 15 and 17.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Tobacco, alcohol, drunkenness, cannabis, other drug use, self-rated health, life satisfaction/Cantril Ladder, sex, age cohort, school type.
Notes / uncertainties: Publication confirms data availability for Lombardy HBSC 2021/22 but paper is not a codebook; exact questionnaire wording and variable names needed.

Dataset name: Ontario Student Drug Use and Health Survey
Access type: Study/data-owner route
Gambling measure summary: Report confirms online gambling, gambling while playing video games/loot boxes/in-game currency/skins betting, and other gambling in grades 7-12; questionnaire/codebook still needed for exact variable names.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Mental health, wellbeing, bullying/cyberbullying, physical health, sleep, screen time, video gaming, social media, school climate, sex, grade, region.
Notes / uncertainties: 2023 report confirms data availability and published indicators but is not a codebook; exact item wording and variable names still need questionnaire/data dictionary.

Dataset name: Manitoba Longitudinal Study of Young Adults
Access type: Repository access
Notes / uncertainties: Potentially useful for gambling, psychosocial and substance-use risk/protective factors.

Dataset name: Understanding Society / UK Household Longitudinal Study
Owner / data controller: Institute for Social and Economic Research, University of Essex; UKDS
Age focus / population: Adults and household members aged 16+; includes 16â€“24 subset
Access type: UKDS / controlled variants for special licence
Gambling measure summary: Wave 13 self-completion gambling module; online gambling variable confirmed; PGSI/participation to verify
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Mental health, wellbeing, sleep, household, income, employment, family, ethnicity, area, education, substance-related variables
Seed/source status: Pre-identified in evidence review; archive search confirmed module
Notes / uncertainties: Good adult/emerging-adult comparator; under-18 not covered in adult self-completion

Dataset name: British Gambling Prevalence Survey
Access type: UKDS registered access
Gambling measure summary: Gambling participation, frequency, mode, volume; problem gambling screens in documentation to verify
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Demographics, health/substance and socioeconomic factors depending year
Notes / uncertainties: Useful for trend/older 16â€“24 analyses; age and measures comparable to later adult surveys to assess

Dataset name: Health Survey for England gambling modules
Access type: UKDS registered access
Gambling measure summary: Gambling participation and PGSI in 2021; earlier years may include PGSI/DSM variants
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Physical/mental health, alcohol, smoking, BMI, socioeconomic and inequality variables
Notes / uncertainties: Strong health/risk-factor data; harmonisation across years and UK nations needs care

Dataset name: Gambling in England and Scotland, 2016: Combined HSE/SHeS
Owner / data controller: NHS Digital / Scottish Government / NatCen; UKDS
Access type: UKDS registered access
Gambling measure summary: Gambling participation and problem/at-risk gambling measures
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Health, lifestyle, demographics, socioeconomic variables
Notes / uncertainties: Useful harmonised England/Scotland comparator

Dataset name: National Survey for Wales gambling module
Age focus / population: Adults aged 16+ in UKDS study; gambling report aged 18+ for 2022/23
Access type: UKDS registered access
Gambling measure summary: Gambling activity/behaviours in previous 12 months; measure details to extract from data dictionary
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Demographics, deprivation, wellbeing, health, household and area variables
Notes / uncertainties: Under-25 subsample likely small; variable-level detail pending

Dataset name: Growing Up in Ireland Cohort '98
Owner / data controller: Economic and Social Research Institute / Irish Social Sciences Data Archive
Age focus / population: Young people followed from childhood; gambling questions at ages 17/18 and 20
Access type: ISSDA application / data request
Gambling measure summary: Online gambling and regular gambling; exact item wording in questionnaires
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Mental health/wellbeing (e.g. CES-D/DASS components), socioeconomic, family, education, health behaviours
Notes / uncertainties: Good longitudinal comparator for 17/18 to 20; variable names/data access to extract

Dataset name: Gambling Impact and Behavior Study
Owner / data controller: National Opinion Research Center / ICPSR NAHDAP
Study design: Cross-sectional adult and youth survey plus community files
Age focus / population: Adults and youth in the US; youth sample includes adolescents
Access type: ICPSR/NAHDAP access
Gambling measure summary: Gambling behaviour/attitudes and problem-gambling diagnostic assessment
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Mental health/suicide/substance and demographics according to documentation; limited longitudinal utility
Notes / uncertainties: Historical US comparator; less relevant for current digital gambling

Dataset name: National Survey on Drug Use and Health
Access type: ICPSR/SAMHSA public and restricted access
Gambling measure summary: ICPSR variable search found lifetime gambling problems variable; gambling module coverage needs confirmation by year
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Very rich substance use and mental health variables; socioeconomic/demographic measures
Notes / uncertainties: Likely useful for youth 12+ if gambling variable/year confirmed; not a gambling-focused survey

Dataset name: Scottish Health Survey 2017/2018/2019/2021
Owner / data controller: Scottish Government / ScotCen / UK Data Service
Age focus / population: Age 16+ adults; includes 16-24 young adults; household and dietary files included
Metadata / data profile URL: Uploaded UK Data Archive data dictionary
Access type: Safeguarded/End User Licence raw data; public metadata in uploaded dictionary
Access difficulty: Metadata available; raw data requires UK Data Service access
Gambling measure summary: Gambling variables extracted where present from uploaded data dictionary. If none detected, dataset retained for risk/protective covariates and access metadata.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Mental health/wellbeing, substance use, socioeconomic position/inequalities, family/household, digital environment, diet/physical health variables where present.
Notes / uncertainties: Uploaded UK Data Archive data dictionary: shes17181921i_eul_ukda_data_dictionary.rtf; file shes17181921i_eul; 1780 variables; 25128 cases.
Source URLs: Uploaded file in current ChatGPT workspace

Dataset name: Gambling, Saving, and Lumpy Liquidity Needs
Access type: Open/public download
Gambling measure summary: Individual-level sports betting dataset from Kampala, Uganda. 1,708 sports bettors surveyed across three phases (September 2015 - July 2016). Includes betting demand, targeted payouts, winnings, and a randomised savings treatment that reduced betting demand by 28%. Two lab-in-the-field experiments test liquidity needs and saving capacity as causal mechanisms.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Economic circumstances, liquidity constraints, savings, income, consumption and financial behaviour; useful socioeconomic and financial-context variables.
Notes / uncertainties: Replication data for Herskowitz (2021) 'Gambling, Saving, and Lumpy Liquidity Needs', American Economic Journal: Applied Economics, 13(1), 72-104. DOI: 10.1257/app.20180177. Data collected September 2015 - July 2016. Three phases of data collection with ~500 respondents each in first two phases creating nine-week panels of reported betting, earning, and consumption.

Dataset name: Access to Healthy Assets and Hazards
Owner / data controller: Consumer Data Research Centre / Geographic Data Service
Age focus / population: Not individual participant data; area-level contextual linkage to cohorts/areas
Access type: Open/public download
Gambling measure summary: Area-level index of access to unhealthy and healthy assets including gambling venues. The Retail Services domain includes gambling outlets alongside fast food, pubs/bars, and vape/tobacco retailers. Covers ~1.7 million postcodes at LSOA/Data Zone level for 2021/22 census boundaries. Not an individual-level gambling survey - provides neighbourhood gambling-environment exposure for contextual linkage.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Built environment, retail environment (gambling outlets, fast food, alcohol/tobacco retail), health services (GPs, pharmacies, hospitals, dentists, leisure centres), green/blue space accessibility, air quality (NO2, PM10, SO2). Four domains combined into composite AHAH score.
Seed/source status: Added at user request as auxiliary/contextual dataset
Notes / uncertainties: AHAH v5 released February 2026. Python toolkit available on GitHub. Higher AHAH scores indicate less healthy environments. Based on Daras et al. (2019) 'Open data on health-related neighbourhood features in Great Britain'. Data sources include NHS Digital, NHS Scotland, DEFRA, Ordnance Survey, OpenStreetMap, and Gambling Commission data. Useful for linking area-level gambling exposure to individual-level cohort data.

Dataset name: 1958 National Child Development Study
Owner / data controller: Centre for Longitudinal Studies, UCL / ESRC
Age focus / population: Born March 1958; sweep 4 (age 23) contains gambling item
Sample size: 17,415 at birth; augmented childhood target sample 18,558; achieved sample at sweep 4 / age 23 = 12,537 cohort members.
Questionnaire URL(s): NCDS Age 23 Annotated Questionnaire Documentation (via UKDS SN 5566)
Access type: UKDS registration; most data freely available
Gambling measure summary: Single gambling participation/frequency item at age 23 (n5919): 'Gambled (including bingo) in past 4 weeks' with 6-point frequency scale.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Verified via CLS metadata and sweep documentation: mental health/depression (Malaise at age 23 and later), smoking and alcohol, family formation and relationships, socioeconomic position/income/housing/employment, education/qualifications, leisure and social participation, physical health. Childhood sweeps add family background, school, cognitive/educational attainment and behaviour/social adjustment.
Notes / uncertainties: Gambling item is in sweep 4 only (age 23, 1981/82). Retrospective adult data: participants aged 23 at gambling measurement. No validated gambling harm scale; single participation/frequency item only. Age 23 achieved sample verified from CLS Age 23 Sweep page.
Source URLs: https://cls.ucl.ac.uk/cls-studies/1958-national-child-development-study/ ; https://cls-data.ucl.ac.uk/explore/variable-search ; https://ukdataservice.ac.uk/ (SN 5566 for sweep 4) ; https://discovery.closer.ac.uk/ ; https://ukdataservice.ac.uk/ ; clsdata@ucl.ac.uk

Dataset name: 1970 British Cohort Study
Owner / data controller: Centre for Longitudinal Studies, UCL / ESRC
Age focus / population: Born April 1970; sweep 4 (age 15/16) contains gambling items
Sample size: 17,196 at birth including 628 Northern Ireland births not followed in later sweeps; Great Britain cohort 16,568 at birth; achieved sample at sweep 4 / age 16 = 11,622 cohort members.
Catalogue URL: https://ukdataservice.ac.uk/ (SN 3535 for sweep 4 main; SN 8949 for leisure diary)
Questionnaire URL(s): BCS70 Age 16 Survey - Documents G and Q (Student Self-Completion Questionnaires) (via UKDS SN 3535)
Access type: UKDS registration; most data freely available
Gambling measure summary: Direct gambling items at age 15/16: (1) q22.10 'Gone into a betting shop' with frequency scale; (2) gg6.14 'Betting/gambling' as one option in question G6 on what the cohort member would spend extra weekly money on, with tick-all and top-three ranking.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Verified via CLS metadata and sweep documentation: mental health/depression (Malaise first included at age 16), smoking, alcohol and other health behaviours, relationships with family and friends, school/headteacher/teacher data, educational and cognitive assessments, family income/housing/parental circumstances, leisure activities, law/risk-taking, beliefs and aspirations. Later sweeps add employment, income, qualifications, mental/physical health, partnership/family and substance-use data.
Notes / uncertainties: Gambling items at age 15/16 (1986). Direct under-18 gambling data. No validated gambling harm scale. q22.10 is a direct betting-shop participation/frequency item. gg6.14 wording and context verified from CLS/UKDS Document G: spending preference and ranking question.
Source URLs: https://cls.ucl.ac.uk/cls-studies/1970-british-cohort-study/ ; https://cls-data.ucl.ac.uk/explore/variable-search ; https://ukdataservice.ac.uk/ (SN 3535 for sweep 4 main ; SN 8949 for leisure diary) ; https://discovery.closer.ac.uk/ ; https://ukdataservice.ac.uk/ ; clsdata@ucl.ac.uk

Dataset name: NSW Youth Gambling Study 2020
Owner / data controller: NSW Responsible Gambling Fund / Office of Responsible Gambling
Study design: Cross-sectional mixed-methods (literature review, focus groups, online survey)
Sample size: 2,220 survey respondents; 104 focus group participants across 16 groups in 8 NSW locations
Access type: Published report; data access route unclear
Access difficulty: Report publicly available; underlying data access unclear
Gambling measure summary: Past-year monetary gambling participation (29.8%), games with gambling components (40.1%), problem gambling rate (1.5%), at-risk gamblers (2.2%). Covers age of initiation, participation rates in monetary and simulated gambling, social contexts of gambling.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Advertising exposure, parental influence on gambling, social contexts of gambling, peer influences, digital/simulated gambling engagement
Seed/source status: Added from GambleAware NSW published research
Notes / uncertainties: Published 2020. Authors: Hing N, Russell A, King D, Rockloff M, Browne M, Greer N, Newall P, Sproston K, Chen L, Coughlin S. Survey recruited via household flyers, Qualtrics panel, and digital advertising. Literature review covered peer-reviewed and grey literature 2003-2019. 16 focus groups across 8 NSW locations. Baseline study for NSWYGS_2024 longitudinal follow-up.

Dataset name: NSW Longitudinal Youth Gambling Study 2024
Study design: Longitudinal mixed-methods (systematic review, two-wave survey, qualitative interviews)
Age focus / population: Young people aged 12-17 at baseline (2020), 16-21 at follow-up (2024); qualitative sample aged 18-23
Sample size: 229 longitudinal survey participants (re-surveyed from 2020 baseline); 50 qualitative interview participants aged 18-23
Access type: Published report; data access route unclear
Access difficulty: Report publicly available; underlying data access unclear
Gambling measure summary: Participants classified as non-gambling, simulated gambling only, monetary gambling, or at-risk/problem gambling at each wave. 44% engaged in simulated gambling in 2020; by 2024, 40% of simulated gamblers transitioned to monetary gambling (6% into at-risk/problem). Approximately 13% reported at-risk/problem gambling in both years; 70% showed consistency across waves. First longitudinal gambling study with young people in NSW.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Transitions from simulated to monetary gambling, early family exposure to gambling, peer influences, advertising influences, turning 18 and legal gambling access, at-risk and problem gambling trajectories
Seed/source status: Added from GambleAware NSW published research
Notes / uncertainties: Published 2025. Authors: Hing N, Lole L, Dellosa G, Tulloch C, Browne M, Russell AMT, Rockloff M. Three components: (1) systematic review of longitudinal youth gambling research 1995-2024, (2) two-wave longitudinal survey of 229 participants surveyed in 2020 (ages 12-17) and again in 2024 (ages 16-21), (3) qualitative interviews with 50 people aged 18-23 in NSW. First longitudinal gambling research conducted with young people in NSW. Builds on NSWYGS_2020 baseline study.

Dataset name: GambleAware Annual GB Treatment and Support Survey
Age focus / population: Adults aged 18+; last two waves (2023, 2024) include retrospective questions about gambling before age 18
Access type: Safeguarded data; application via GeoDS/CDRC or UKDS
Access difficulty: Metadata open; raw data safeguarded application
Gambling measure summary: PGSI (Problem Gambling Severity Index) with PGSI 1+ (any problems) and PGSI 8+ (problem gambling) thresholds. Gambling participation across product types, frequency, spending. Gambling harms. Demand for and uptake of treatment, support and advice. Affected others (people affected by another person's gambling). Last two waves (2023-2024) include retrospective items on gambling before age 18.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Demographics (age, gender, region, social grade, ethnic group), mental health (linked analysis by Alma Economics 2023), substance use (pre-18 retrospective measures in 2023/2024 waves), socioeconomic position, advertising exposure, stigma, cost-of-living impacts, affected others
Seed/source status: Added from user-provided link to UKDS SN 9536
Notes / uncertainties: Conducted annually by YouGov since 2019; one of the largest gambling harm surveys worldwide. Data weighted by age, gender, UK region, social grade and ethnic group. LSOA-level geographies available for 2019, 2020, 2022 waves. Variables per wave: 962 (2022), 924 (2020), 242 (2019 P1), 736 (2019 P2). Data format CSV. Supplemented by ~30 qualitative interviews/focus groups per wave. Aggregated data tables (Excel) publicly available 2021-2024. Local area data profiles available 2022-2024.

Dataset name: Australian Secondary School Alcohol and Drug Survey
Owner / data controller: Cancer Council Victoria / state health departments
Study design: Cross-sectional school survey (triennial since 1984; gambling module added 2017)
Sample size: 6,377 students from 93 schools (57 Victoria, 36 Queensland) in 2017 gambling module
Access type: Available from corresponding author on reasonable request
Gambling measure summary: DSM-IV-MR-J (12 items, dichotomous yes/no). Gambling participation (ever, past 30 days), types (horse/dog races, sports betting, cards, lotteries, poker machines), modality (online, app, parent-facilitated, venue), problem gambling classification (non-problem, at-risk, problem).
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Demographics (gender, age, SES, geography, language), substance use (alcohol, tobacco, illicit drugs), mental health, money available, school achievement, peer/family gambling, gambling advertising/promotion exposure, venue attendance
Notes / uncertainties: ASSAD has run since 1984 covering alcohol, tobacco and drugs among Australian school students; gambling module first added in 2017. Publication: Freund et al., 2022, J Gambling Studies. DOI: 10.1007/s10899-021-10098-z

Dataset name: Youth'07 - New Zealand Youth Health and Wellbeing Survey
Owner / data controller: University of Auckland Adolescent Health Research Group
Access type: Published open access; data access route unclear
Access difficulty: Report publicly available; underlying data access unclear
Gambling measure summary: Custom gambling items: past-year gambling, gambling activities (Lotto, pokies, casino, internet, TAB betting), frequency, money spent weekly, time spent daily, reasons for gambling (13 options including 'can't stop', 'feel better about myself'). No standard named instrument (PGSI/SOGS-RA) used.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Depression (Reynolds Adolescent Depression Scale-Short Form), wellbeing (WHO-5), social connectedness (family, friends, school), weekly alcohol use, weekly income, demographics (age, sex, ethnicity), neighbourhood deprivation
Notes / uncertainties: Data collected in 2007. Publication: Rossen et al., 2016, Asian J Gambling Issues Public Health. DOI: 10.1186/s40405-016-0017-9. Part of the Youth2000 survey series (Youth2000, Youth'07, Youth'12).

Dataset name: Canadian Youth Gambling Survey
Study design: Cross-sectional school-based survey (supplement to Canadian Youth Smoking Survey)
Access type: Open access publication; data access route unclear
Access difficulty: Report publicly available; underlying data access unclear
Gambling measure summary: Canadian Adolescent Gambling Inventory (CAGI) with Gambling Problem Severity Subscale (GPSS, 9 items). 16 types of gambling for money (past 3 months). Free simulated gambling (internet poker, slots, Facebook games). Online vs land-based.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Gender, age, multiple gambling participation patterns, problem gambling severity (no problem/low-moderate/high)
Notes / uncertainties: Data collected 2012-2013 as supplement to Youth Smoking Survey. Publication: Elton-Marshall et al., 2016, BMC Public Health. DOI: 10.1186/s12889-016-2933-0. See also Derevensky and Gupta's works on Canadian youth gambling.

Dataset name: Canadian Community Health Survey: Mental Health and Well-Being (youth gambling component)
Access type: Statistics Canada data access (likely Research Data Centre or public-use microdata file)
Gambling measure summary: Canadian Problem Gambling Index (CPGI). Past 12-month gambling participation (61.35% youth). Moderate-risk or problem gambling (national 2.22%; males 3.30%; females 1.10%). Regional variations across provinces.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Sex, geographic region (BC, Prairies, Ontario, Quebec, Atlantic provinces)
Notes / uncertainties: Publication: Huang & Boyer, 2007, Canadian J Psychiatry. DOI: 10.1177/070674370705201006. National-level youth gambling prevalence data using CPGI.

Dataset name: US National Gambling Survey - Youth Component (Welte et al.)
Owner / data controller: Research Institute on Addictions, University at Buffalo
Access type: Published in J Gambling Studies; data access route unclear
Gambling measure summary: SOGS-RA (South Oaks Gambling Screen-Revised for Adolescents). Problem gambling prevalence 2.1%. Past-year gambling 68%. 11% gambled more than twice per week.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Gender, race/ethnicity (Black respondents), socioeconomic status, life transitions (employment, independent living, student status)
Notes / uncertainties: Publication: Welte et al., 2008, J Gambling Studies. DOI: 10.1007/s10899-007-9086-0. One of few US national-level youth gambling prevalence studies.

Dataset name: Korean National Survey on Youth Gambling Problems
Access type: Korean Center on Gambling Problems (secondary analysis with IRB approval)
Gambling measure summary: CAGI/GPSS (Gambling Problem Severity Scale, 9 of 24 CAGI items, 4-point Likert, 0-27 points). Lifetime and past 3-month gambling experience, number of gambling behaviours, online vs offline, frequency, time spent, money spent/lost/won, age of onset.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Gender, age, residential area, personal relationships with gamblers, nearby gambling facility accessibility
Notes / uncertainties: Data collected Aug-Oct 2015. Publication: Kang et al., 2019, Int J Environ Res Public Health. DOI: 10.3390/ijerph16122110.

Dataset name: Finnish School Health Promotion Study
Owner / data controller: Finnish Institute for Health and Welfare (THL)
Access type: Data available from THL with restrictions; not publicly available but accessible upon request
Gambling measure summary: Weekly gambling frequency ('How often do you gamble?' with frequency categories: 1-2, 3-5, 6-7 days per week).
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Substance use (heavy episodic drinking, daily smoking, lifetime drug use), sports activity, school orientation, health perception, life satisfaction, peer relationships (close friendships), family communication, financial situation, youth origin/migration status
Notes / uncertainties: Very large sample. Publication: Raitasalo et al., 2024, J Gambling Studies. DOI: 10.1007/s10899-024-10321-7. Focus on youth origin/migration as risk factor. See also AHLS (Adolescent Health and Lifestyle Survey) for complementary Finnish data.

Dataset name: Adolescent Health and Lifestyle Survey (AHLS)
Owner / data controller: Tampere University, Faculty of Social Sciences
Access type: Tampere University (likely restricted/application-based)
Gambling measure summary: Gambling behaviour items included in survey. Also used in Kinnunen et al. studies on adolescent gambling, gambling expenditure and gambling-related harms (2011-2017 data).
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Tobacco, alcohol, gambling, health/stress symptoms, physical activity, diet, dental hygiene, technology use, contraception, sleep, violence exposure, obesity
Notes / uncertainties: PI: Prof Arja Rimpela (Tampere University). Survey conducted biennially 1977-2019. Team includes Pirjo Lindfors and Jaana Kinnunen. See Kinnunen et al. on adolescent gambling expenditure and harms (DOI: 10.1007/s10899-019-09892-7).

Dataset name: Swedish Council for Information on Alcohol and Other Drugs (CAN) Annual School Survey
Study design: Cross-sectional school survey (annual since 1971 for grade 9; since 2005 for grade 11)
Access type: CAN (data access route to verify)
Gambling measure summary: Nine gambling items (poker, vending machines, sports betting, lotteries, online gambling). Four-point frequency scale (never to several times per week). Dichotomised: 1-11 times vs 12+ times past 12 months. Semi-continuous annual frequency (0-468 scale).
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Gender, family satisfaction, school satisfaction, bullying (perpetration and victimisation), truancy, alcohol consumption, heavy episodic drinking, drug use, economic situation
Notes / uncertainties: 2014 wave analysed. Annual survey since 1971. Paper-and-pen classroom questionnaires; response rates 82-85%. Publication: Svensson & Sundqvist, 2019, Nordisk Alkohol- & Narkotikatidskrift. DOI: 10.1177/1455072518807788.

Dataset name: Italian Population Survey on Alcohol and Drugs (IPSAD-Italia)
Access type: CNR/Springer (data access route to verify)
Gambling measure summary: Canadian Problem Gambling Index Short Form. Gambling participation, low-risk gambling, moderate-risk/problem gambling. Gender differences.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Gender, education level (primary vs university), depressant use (youth), attitudes toward gambling
Notes / uncertainties: Data from 2007-2008 wave. Publication: Bastiani et al., 2013, J Gambling Studies. DOI: 10.1007/s10899-011-9283-8. IPSAD is a recurring Italian population survey.

Dataset name: Hong Kong High School Internet Gambling Study
Access type: Published in J Gambling Studies; data access route unclear
Gambling measure summary: DSM-IV-MR-J (12 items). Perceptions of Internet Gambling Questionnaire (PIGQ, 7 items). Internet Gambling Behavior Questionnaire. Online/offline participation, problem gambling severity (social, at-risk, pathological), frequency, amounts wagered, preferred types (mahjong, poker, casino, sports, lotteries, horse racing), age of initiation.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Gender, school grades, family gambling environment, internet usage patterns/hours, peer influence, family member gambling, gambling motivations/perceptions
Notes / uncertainties: Data collected 2010. Publication: Wong & So, 2014, J Gambling Studies. DOI: 10.1007/s10899-013-9413-6. Note: user originally linked PMID 22138931 for Hong Kong but that is the Italy IPSAD paper; this PMC4141147 study is the correct Hong Kong reference.

Dataset name: Internet Gambling Among Macau Students
Access type: Published paper; data access route unclear
Gambling measure summary: Massachusetts Gambling Screen (MAGS). 6.6% gambled online past year. Internet gambling on soccer matches (50%), mahjong (35.7%), casino games (14.3%). 10.7% problem and 25% pathological among internet gamblers.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Gender (males 2x more likely), school grade, anonymity/accessibility preferences
Notes / uncertainties: Published 2010 in Social Behavior and Personality, 38(3), 365-371. Smaller sample but unique Macau context with legal gambling culture.

Dataset name: European Network for Addictive Behaviour (EUNETADB) Study
Country: Multi-country: Germany, Greece, Iceland, Netherlands, Poland, Romania, Spain
Owner / data controller: EU Safer Internet Programme / EUNETADB consortium
Age focus / population: Adolescents (exact age range not specified; school-based)
Access type: EUNETADB consortium (data access route to verify)
Gambling measure summary: SOGS-RA (South Oaks Gambling Screen-Revised for Adolescents). Last-year gambling participation (online/offline), problem gambling, at-risk gambling. Also: Internet Addiction Test, Achenbach Youth Self-Report.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Gender, age, parental educational level, sibling status, internet use frequency/patterns, school performance, problematic internet use, behavioural competencies
Notes / uncertainties: Funded by EU Safer Internet Programme (SI-2011-KEP-4101007). Separate from ESPAD. Publication: Andrie et al., 2019, Soc Psychiatry Psychiatr Epidemiol. DOI: 10.1007/s00127-019-01706-w.

Dataset name: Spanish Adolescent Gambling Longitudinal Study (Alicante)
Owner / data controller: UNED / Universidad Miguel Hernandez de Elche
Age focus / population: Students aged 13-18 (mean 15.12 at T1, 16.07 at T2)
Sample size: 1,074 paired participants (T1: 2,716; T2: 2,430) from 15 high schools
Access type: Open access publication; data access route unclear
Gambling measure summary: SOGS-RA (12 dichotomous items + 1 Likert; alpha=0.72). ESPAD-adapted gambling frequency items across 8 modes (online sports betting, sports parlors/bars, slot machines, online poker, poker with friends, online casino, roulette, peer betting). Frequency across lifetime/12 months/30 days. Classification: non-gambler/nonproblematic, at-risk (2-3), problematic (4+).
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Impulsivity (Plutchik 15-item), sensation-seeking (BSSS-8), risk perception (EDGAR-A), self-efficacy (8-item refuse gambling), parental attitudes (EDGAR-A), parents' gambling, peer pressure, subjective norm (parents/friends/classmates/teachers/partner), normative perception, accessibility (EDGAR-A), media/advertising exposure (12-item across TV/internet/radio/magazines/outdoor)
Notes / uncertainties: Data collected 2016-2017. 55.12% female in paired sample. Three predictive models for gambling initiation identified. Publication: Botella-Guijarro et al., 2020, IJERPH. DOI: 10.3390/ijerph17249266.

Dataset name: From Adolescence to Young Adulthood: Simulated and Traditional Gambling (VRGF)
Study design: Cross-sectional online survey with retrospective age-of-onset data
Age focus / population: Young adults aged 18-25 who had played video games in past year
Sample size: 1,026 (half Victoria, half rest of Australia; ~60% female; ~80% Australian-born)
Access type: VRGF commissioned report (CC BY licence); data access route unclear
Access difficulty: Report publicly available; underlying data access unclear
Gambling measure summary: PGSI (9 items, alpha=0.94) for past 12-month problem gambling severity. NODS-CLiP (3 items, KR-20=0.60) for lifetime gambling problems. Gambling Harms Scale (GHS-10, 10 items, alpha=0.86). Simulated gambling engagement: video games with gambling content, loot boxes (free and paid), social casino games. Traditional gambling engagement: lifetime and past 12-month monetary gambling by type. Age of first participation for each simulated and monetary form.
Key risk/protective domains confirmed: Parental gambling behaviour, parental attitudes to gambling, family and friend influences on gambling, simulated gambling as gateway to monetary gambling, age of initiation, gender
Seed/source status: Added from user-provided VRGF report link
Notes / uncertainties: Published April 2023 (report says February 2022 on cover). Commissioned by VRGF. Authors: Russell A, Hing N, Newall PWS, Greer N, Dittman C. Data collected late 2020. CC BY licence. Related papers: Hing et al. 2022 IJERPH (PMID 36078295); Russell et al. 2023 J Behavioral Addictions (PMC10786233). Persistent ID: hdl.handle.net/1983/78d1619b-f7ef-4d43-896a-0a802e9388ba
